full_log: CASE 1 — marginal overbid of human partner: blocked
P2 (player_id=2, partner_id=0) vs P0's 30-point open, human_seat=0
hand target_bid=31 — clears the OLD bar (min_points=31) but not the
NEW one (required_target = min_points+3 = 34)
PASS: case1_gate_blocks_marginal_overbid — expected PASS, got Pass
CASE 2 — strong overbid of human partner: gate clears, filter composes
Same setup, hand target_bid=34. Worked example: min_points=31,
required_target=34, gate clears (34>=34), _announced_points_bid(34,31,...)
-> min_points+1=32 at standard difficulty (max_overbid=4). Logged for
sanity, NOT asserted on directly — see file header re: test 2.
independently computed via _announced_points_bid(target_bid=34, min_points=31, ...) = 32
PASS: case2_gate_clears — expected a POINTS bid (gate cleared), got 32 points

PASS: case2_composition_uses_min_points_not_required_target — decide_bid()'s announced value (32 points) must equal _announced_points_bid() called with the real min_points=31 (got 32 independently) — proves min_points, not required_target=34, was passed through
CASE 3 — non-partner overbid: gate doesn't fire
Same hand that failed case 1 (target_bid=31), but current_high is now
held by P1 (an opponent to P2's team), not P0 — ordinary min_points gate
PASS: case3_gate_ignores_non_partner_overbid — expected an ordinary POINTS bid (old min_points-only gate applies), got 31 points
CASE 4 — partner isn't human: gate doesn't fire even though
current_high.player_id == partner_id
Testing from P1's perspective (partner_id = (1+2)%4 = 3). current_high
is held by P3 — P1's REAL partner — but human_seat=0 (not 3), so
partner_id != human_seat and the scope restriction must hold.
PASS: case4_gate_scoped_to_human_partner_only — expected an ordinary POINTS bid (partner_id != human_seat, gate inapplicable), got 31 points
CASE 5 — is_forced sanity re-check (already covered by
bid_filter_trace.gd; re-confirmed here after this change)
is_forced implies current_high == null, so overbidding_human_partner
can never be true — this scenario cannot reach the new gate at all.
PASS: case5_forced_bid_unaffected — expected POINTS(30), got 30 points
CASE 6 — statelessness: a conservative announcement doesn't cap
the private evaluation on a later call
Step A: human opens 30. P2 (target_bid=34) clears the gate, announces
conservatively (case 2, above).
Step B: an opponent raises to 33. decide_bid() called again for P2,
same hand -> target_bid recomputes fresh to 34 (stateless, nothing
persisted). Expect P2 still willing to raise.
independently computed via _announced_points_bid(target_bid=34, min_points=34, ...) = 34
PASS: case6_still_willing_to_raise — expected P2 to still raise with a POINTS bid, got 34 points

PASS: case6_composition_consistent — decide_bid()'s announced value (34 points) must equal independently-computed _announced_points_bid() with min_points=34 (got 34) — confirms target_bid was recomputed fresh, not capped by the earlier conservative announcement
ALL CHECKS PASSED
